title: Roadmap follow-ups: TUI roadmap selection, cross-epic graph blockers, PR+CI gate adoption
description:
Final epic in the soft-ordering roadmap (4bt -> o16 -> this). Collects the follow-ups proposed in the kgh (Execution Model v2 Phase 4) retro that were never ticked, plus the pre-existing cross-epic graph tick iga.

Sequencing note — this epic is itself a live example of the preference-vs-dependency distinction the roadmap ships: the epic-level blocked_by o16 edge is pure PREFERENCE (deliver surfaces before follow-ups) and is the canonical candidate to convert to the after field once 4bt lands. The one real technical coupling is carried explicitly at task level instead: zlc is blocked_by o16 because both modify internal/tui/roadmap.go (same-file sequencing is a hard dependency per tick-patterns doctrine). iga and h3l are technically independent of the rest of the roadmap and intentionally workable in parallel with it — children do not inherit epic blockers, which here is a feature.

Children are fully ticked up front at user request (deviation from the parent-only convention for downstream epics — scope here is already well understood):
- zlc: TUI roadmap-native selection (kgh retro follow-up; TUI roadmap is display-only v1). Hard-blocked by o16 (shared TUI files).
- iga (existing tick, re-parented): tk graph must account for cross-epic blockers.
- h3l: PR+CI gate adoption via .tick/config.md (kgh retro follow-up; CI is now wired — .github/workflows/ci.yml). Gated --requires approval: it sets project policy.
Plus planning-time meta-work ticks (0dq final review, yj6 close-out). This is the last epic in the roadmap, so its close-out is retro-only.
